svg.title: Fitness tracker layered monolith architecture
svg.desc: Four backend layers (Presentation, Application, Domain, Infrastructure) connected to a React frontend and PostgreSQL database

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Walk me through the React frontend: routing, state management, and the custom FitnessTable component wrapping TanStack')
sendPrompt('Show me the API routes breakdown: auth routes, client routes, and admin routes with their endpoints')
sendPrompt('What Pydantic v2 schemas do we need for request validation and response serialization?')
sendPrompt('What middleware layers do we need: JWT validation, role guards, CORS config?')
sendPrompt('Tell me about all 5 services: AuthService, ClientService, AdminService, WorkoutService, DietService — what does each own?')
sendPrompt('What do WorkoutFactory and DietFactory do — why do we need factories here?')
sendPrompt('What kind of use-case logic lives in the application layer versus the domain layer?')
sendPrompt('Show me the domain entities: User, WorkoutPlan, WorkoutEntry, DietPlan, DietEntry — as pure Python dataclasses')
sendPrompt('What abstract repository and service interfaces does the domain layer define as contracts?')
sendPrompt('Show me the concrete repository implementations for UserRepository, WorkoutRepository, DietRepository')
sendPrompt('How do SQLAlchemy ORM models differ from domain entities, and how do repositories map between them?')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the async database session singleton and how Alembic migrations work in this project')
sendPrompt('Show me the Docker Compose setup for PostgreSQL, including environment variables and volume configuration')
